questions.question: What is a decorator in TypeScript?
options: B) A special function that can modify or annotate classes, methods, and properties
C) A type alias for object properties
D) A way to format console output

questions.question: What is the syntax for applying a decorator to a class?
In the TC39 (Stage 3) decorator proposal, what arguments does a class decorator receive?
options: A) The class name and prototype
B) The target class and a context object

questions.question: What is the Builder pattern?
options: A) A pattern for building npm packages
B) A creational pattern that constructs complex objects step-by-step with a fluent interface
C) A pattern for building TypeScript types
D) A pattern for constructing HTML
B) A User object with name, age, and email set

questions.question: How does the Builder pattern enable method chaining?
options: B) By returning 'this' from each setter method

questions.question: What is the Singleton pattern?
options: A) A class that can only have one method
B) A pattern ensuring a class has exactly one instance, with global access to it
D) A pattern for handling single values

questions.question: How is a Singleton typically implemented in TypeScript?
options: B) With a private constructor and a static getInstance() method

questions.question: What is a mapped type in TypeScript?
options: A) A type that maps strings to numbers
B) A type that transforms each property of an existing type using a mapping syntax
D) A type for geographic coordinates

questions.question:
What does this mapped type produce?

type Readonly<T> = { readonly [K in keyof T]: T[K] };
options: A) Removes all properties from T
B) Makes every property of T readonly
C) Makes T a string type
D) Adds new properties to T

questions.question:
What does this mapped type produce?

type Partial<T> = { [K in keyof T]?: T[K] };
options: A) Removes all properties from T
B) Makes every property of T optional
D) Converts T to a union type

questions.question: What is a conditional type in TypeScript?
options: A) A type that changes at runtime
B) A type that selects one of two types based on a condition: T extends U ? X : Y
C) A type for if/else statements
D) A type that conditionally compiles code

questions.question: What is a template literal type?
options: A) A template for creating string variables
B) A type that uses template literal syntax to create string pattern types
C) A type for HTML templates

questions.question:
What does this template literal type produce?

type Greeting = `Hello, ${string}`;
options: B) A type matching any string that starts with 'Hello, '

questions.question: What does the 'infer' keyword do in conditional types?
options: A) Infers the type of a variable
B) Introduces a type variable to be inferred from the extends clause
C) Makes TypeScript infer all types automatically

questions.question:
What does the '-' modifier do in a mapped type?

type Required<T> = { [K in keyof T]-?: T[K] };
options: A) Subtracts properties from the type
B) Removes the optional (?) modifier, making all properties required

questions.question: What does Record<K, V> produce?
options: A) An array of key-value pairs
B) An object type with keys of type K and values of type V
A) { name: string; age: number; email: string }

questions.question: Why might you use the Singleton pattern for a database connection?
options: A) To make the connection faster
B) To ensure only one connection instance exists, avoiding resource waste

questions.question: What does Exclude<T, U> do?
options: A) Removes properties from an object
B) Removes from union T any members that are assignable to U
D) Excludes a module from imports

questions.question:
What does this resolve to?

type A = Exclude<'a' | 'b' | 'c', 'a'>;
